details: SWISH: compute_kernel=eltwise_sfpu.cpp, init=swish_tile_init(), func=swish_tile(idst), macro=SFPU_OP_SWISH_INCLUDE, approx_mode=false (default)

ATANH: compute kernel=eltwise_sfpu.cpp, SFPU_OP_CHAIN_0 expansion=atanh_tile(0), init=atanh_tile_init(), approx_mode=false (default case), macro=SFPU_OP_ATANH_INCLUDE

No softshrink SFPU kernel found in tt_metal/hw/ckernels or tt_metal/third_party/tt_llk. Checking if implemented as composite or through hardshrink.

TANHSHRINK not in unary_op_utils switch statements (get_compute_kernel_path, get_op_init_and_func_default, get_op_approx_mode). Has dedicated compute kernel tanhshrink_kernel.cpp. Searching for how it is dispatched.

softshrink SFPU kernel has been DELETED from the codebase. DEEP_NUKE_MANIFEST confirms: dispatch removed, compute API deleted, ckernel deleted (wh+bh), LLK deleted (wh+bh). Only the enum type SOFTSHRINK and REGISTER macro still exist as stubs. No softshrink_tile or softshrink_init functions exist anywhere.
HARDTANH has core SFPU impl in ckernel_sfpu_hardtanh.h but NO API header, NO LLK dispatch, and NOT wired in get_op_init_and_func. The operation enum exists but full dispatch chain is incomplete.

API: swish.h -> LLK: llk_math_eltwise_unary_sfpu_swish.h -> Core: ckernel_sfpu_swish.h. Params dispatch via llk_math_eltwise_unary_sfpu_params.h. Both WH and BH implementations identical for the SFPU kernel.

HARDTANH has: 1) UnaryOpType::HARDTANH in enum, 2) is_parametrized_type=true, 3) _calculate_hardtanh_ in ckernel_sfpu_hardtanh.h (WH and BH identical), 4) SfpuType::hardtanh in test helpers only. MISSING: no case in get_op_init_and_func_parameterized, no API header, no LLK dispatch, no SfpuType in production Metal. The operation dispatch chain is incomplete - hardtanh_tile/hardtanh_tile_init do not exist in production code.

key_findings: Uses SFPI abstractions (vFloat, dst_reg, v_if/v_endif, abs). Piecewise sigmoid approximation with polynomial (deg-3), linear segment, and saturation. WH and BH implementations identical.

notes: softshrink SFPU kernel was nuked from codebase. DEEP_NUKE_MANIFEST confirms: dispatch removed, compute API deleted, ckernel deleted (wh+bh), LLK deleted (wh+bh). get_op_init_and_func_parameterized has no case for SOFTSHRINK (would throw TT_THROW at runtime). Only enum type and registration macro remain as stubs.

details: Verified: calculate_atanh (WH+BH), atanh_init (WH+BH), all file paths exist. SFPI-based kernel (21 sfpi:: usages, no raw TTI). Instructions: SFPEXEXP (via exexp), SFPSETEXP (via setexp), SFPMAD (via vFloat arithmetic), SFPLOAD/SFPSTORE (via dst_reg), SFPCAST (via int32_to_float), SFPCONFIG (via vConstFloatPrgm writes in init).

key_findings: softshrink SFPU kernel was completely deleted in Phase 1 deep nuke (commit efdc0ad853). Part of SFPU_OP_ACTIVATIONS_INCLUDE family. The compute API header softshrink.h, ckernel_sfpu_softshrink.h (wh+bh), and llk_math_eltwise_unary_sfpu_softshrink.h (wh+bh) were all deleted. The dispatch path in get_op_init_and_func_parameterized has no SOFTSHRINK case (would TT_THROW). Only residual infrastructure remains: UnaryOpType::SOFTSHRINK enum, is_parametrized_type returns true, REGISTER_UNARY_OPERATION_WITH_FLOAT_PARAMETER macro.

notes: No SFPU kernel exists to analyze. The softshrink operation has been fully nuked from the SFPU layer.

init_func: NOT WIRED - hardtanh_tile_init() does not exist
tile_func: NOT WIRED - hardtanh_tile() does not exist

details: TANHSHRINK is NOT routed through standard SFPU_OP_CHAIN_0 dispatch. It has a dedicated compute kernel tanhshrink_kernel.cpp that uses tanh_tile(0) + binary_dest_reuse_tiles(ELWSUB). The unary_op_utils.cpp does not have TANHSHRINK in get_op_init_and_func_default or get_compute_kernel_path - the standard dispatch would throw. The dedicated kernel exists as an orphan. Will analyze the tanh_tile SFPU path used by the kernel.

Wrote atanh_analysis.md to .claude-analysis/softcap-1/. SFPU kernel uses IEEE 754 decomposition for ln() with cubic minimax polynomial. Key instructions: SFPLOAD, SFPSTORE, SFPMAD (Horner chain), SFPEXEXP, SFPSETEXP, SFPCAST, SFPCONFIG.

cc_pattern: three v_if blocks for piecewise sigmoid + one v_if for sign

key_findings: Uses SFPI abstractions (vFloat, dst_reg, v_if/v_endif). Implements piecewise clamping via 3 additions and 2 conditional zeroing steps. Params in FP16_B: p0=-min_val, p1=-(max_val-min_val), p2=-max_val. WH and BH implementations are identical.

addr_mod: ADDR_MOD_7 (dest.incr=0, standard for generic SFPU ops)

notes: SFPU kernel fully deleted from codebase. Analysis documents deletion and provides re-implementation guidance.

details: llk_math_eltwise_unary_sfpu_tanh is called in compute_kernel_api.h but has NO implementation anywhere in the codebase. No llk_math_eltwise_unary_sfpu_tanh.h exists. No ckernel_sfpu_tanh.h exists. tanh is NOT in the Metal SfpuType enum. The tanhshrink_kernel.cpp calls tanh_tile() which would fail to link. Tanh appears to have been a builtin hardware function whose SFPU LLK binding was removed or never implemented in this version of the code.

This is a modified/eval codebase. Several functions referenced in headers are undefined: llk_math_eltwise_unary_sfpu_tanh, calculate_sfpu_binary (no underscores, 4 template params), sfpu_binary_init. The tt_llk submodule has _calculate_sfpu_binary_ (with underscores, 3 template params). The tanhshrink kernels (both variants) would not compile in this codebase state. Will analyze the available code as-is and document what exists.
